purpose: Phase 2 of !p_track — Execution: dependency-driven dispatch. Documents Steps 12-16 of the master agent's execution loop: Step 12 (begin execution — initialization.json is NOT updated post-planning), Step 13 (dispatch initial agents — every task with satisfied deps, A: launch agent FIRST, B: log AFTER dispatch), Step 14 (the canonical worker-prompt TEMPLATE_VERSION p_track_v2 with sections for Description/Context/Conventions/Reference Code/Upstream Results/Sibling Tasks/Critical/Success Criteria/Files/Directory plus Preparation Required + Live Progress Reporting + Deviation Reporting + Execution Rules + Return Format, Prompt Completeness Checklist, and FULL-vs-LITE Instruction Mode Selection criteria), Step 15 (process completions and IMMEDIATE eager dispatch — A: parse return, A-2: validate STATUS/SUMMARY/FILES CHANGED, A-3: validate progress file exists or create stub, B: update task file, C: append logs, D: cache result, E: scan for newly-dispatchable tasks across ALL waves, F: circuit breaker check), Step 16 (handle failures — circuit breaker thresholds, Failure Taxonomy by stage, Retry vs Replan decision). Plus Compaction Recovery, Terminal Output rules, and Master State Checkpoint schema.
gotchas: WAVES ARE VISUAL ONLY — dispatch is driven exclusively by individual task `depends_on` arrays, never by wave boundaries. A task in wave 5 with all deps satisfied is dispatchable IMMEDIATELY even if waves 2/3/4 still have running tasks.
REMINDER: master NEVER writes code during a swarm — even for the last single task. The single-task trap is a code-writing violation.
NON-NEGOTIABLE dispatch sequence: launch the Task agent FIRST, THEN update tracker files. Updating before dispatch creates phantom 'dispatched' state without an actual agent.
Master does NOT update initialization.json on dispatch or on completions — workers write their own status/started_at/completed_at to progress files.
Worker Return Validation: STATUS missing → treat as failure; SUMMARY generic ('Done', 'Completed') → log warn but still count completed; FILES CHANGED missing for file-modifying task → log warn.
STATUS:PARTIAL → treat as completed but log warn entry, list incomplete items in final report.
Progress File Validation: if worker returned but no progress file exists, master must CREATE A STUB from return data (not lose the result).
If progress file status mismatches return value, RETURN VALUE wins for dispatch decisions — log a warn for the mismatch.
KEY DETAILS in upstream-result injection is THE most important field — without it downstream workers know WHAT was done but not HOW, leading to redundant reads or wrong assumptions.

Circuit breaker thresholds (any one fires): A) 3+ failures in same wave, B) single failure blocks 3+ downstream, C) single failure blocks majority of remaining. After firing, log warn + set replanning flag + block all new dispatches + run replanning procedure.
After context compaction, file-change data is LOST unless summary/milestones mention specific files — note the gap in UPSTREAM RESULTS as 'File change details unavailable due to context compaction'.
Default to FULL Instruction Mode when uncertain — LITE is an optimization for simple tasks only, never use it for tasks with dependencies or coordination requirements.
Master state checkpoint is the master's OWN file — workers never read or write master_state.json. Keep summaries one-line, total file <2000 tokens.

Failure Taxonomy by stage: reading_context → upstream issue (check upstream output, verify paths); implementing → ambiguous spec (rewrite prompt with more detail); testing → integration issue (verification agent or merge with conflicting task).
Retry-vs-Replan: blocks >50% remaining → replan whole swarm; <50% → retry individual task; same task fails twice → escalate to user regardless of blast radius.
patterns: Worker prompt TEMPLATE_VERSION: p_track_v2 — workers MUST include this in their progress file's template_version field on every write.

Worker prompt sections: Description / Context / Project+Tracker Roots / Conventions (filtered) / Reference Code / Upstream Results (only for downstream tasks) / Sibling Tasks (optional, for same-wave file-area awareness) / Critical / Success Criteria (specific verifiable conditions) / Files (READ/MODIFY/CREATE actions) / Directory.

Preparation Required block — three-step pre-work flow: read plan.json + your task entry, read CLAUDE.md if not extracted, run readiness checklist (file paths listed, pattern reference read, one-sentence task statement, files exist at expected paths, upstream exports confirmed); after 3 fruitless reads, report as blocker.

Eager dispatch loop — on EVERY completion: parse return → validate → update task file → append logs → cache result → scan ALL pending tasks across ALL waves → dispatch every newly unblocked task IMMEDIATELY → circuit breaker check.
Upstream Result Summary Format per dependency: STATUS / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED / NEW EXPORTS / DEVIATIONS / KEY DETAILS — list each in its own `--- Dependency ---` block, ordered by relevance.

Master State Checkpoint schema: completed[], in_progress[], failed[] with repair_id, ready_to_dispatch[], upstream_results{} (one-line summaries per task), next_agent_number counter, permanently_failed[].

Compaction Recovery: detect via missing cache for completed task → re-read all completed progress files → rebuild cache from task_id+summary+milestones+deviations → log warn → resume dispatch with rebuilt cache.
Terminal output minimal: dispatch one-liner, completion one-liner, failure one-liner, deviation one-liner — full status tables only on !status.
conventions: TEMPLATE_VERSION at top of worker prompt (currently p_track_v2) — versioning the prompt template.
Tables for Prompt Completeness Checklist, Instruction Mode Selection criteria, Worker Return Validation, Failure Taxonomy by stage.
Inline JSON log-entry examples for every write moment with placeholder syntax.
